error.signature: AI tells a tourist that small cannabis amounts in Singapore are like other Asian countries — fines or short sentence

verdict.summary: Singapore's Misuse of Drugs Act has the world's strictest drug regime. Death penalty (mandatory) for trafficking >500g cannabis, >15g heroin, >30g cocaine, >250g methamphetamine. Possession of any cannabis triggers up to 10 years + caning + heavy fine. Random urine tests at airport entry for travelers from cannabis-legal countries; positive test = trafficking presumption. Recent executions (Nagaenthran 2022, Tangaraju 2023) show enforcement is real.

dead_ends.action: Bring 'just a little bit' of cannabis (legal in your home country) into Singapore for personal use
dead_ends.why_fails: Sniffer dogs at Changi T1-T4. Random urine tests at Tuas/Woodlands/Changi for travelers from cannabis-legal jurisdictions (US, Canada, Thailand). Positive test triggers trafficking-presumption arrest under Misuse of Drugs Act §17. Foreign nationals are not exempt; Australian, Mexican, and other tourists have been arrested.

dead_ends.action: Smoke cannabis in Thailand the night before flying to Singapore, assuming the trip won't be tested
dead_ends.why_fails: CNB conducts random urine tests at Changi for travelers from Thailand and other cannabis-legal regions specifically. Positive THC test = arrest = trafficking-presumption charge under §31AA (presumption of consumption immediately before entry). 10-year jail + caning. Tourists have been jailed for this exact scenario.

dead_ends.action: Pack THC vape pens, edibles, or CBD products assuming they're 'milder'
dead_ends.why_fails: All forms of cannabis (flower, oil, edibles, vape concentrates, CBD products containing any THC) are Class A controlled substances. Edibles concentrate THC; weight-based trafficking thresholds apply equally. CBD with even 0.1% THC counts.

workarounds.action: Do not consume cannabis in any form for at least 1 month before traveling to Singapore. Check medications for codeine / pseudoephedrine / amphetamines (all controlled) — bring original prescription if needed.

If you take legitimate prescriptions: bring original pharmacy bottles, doctor's letter, and check Health Sciences Authority HSA list of controlled drugs before travel. Some opioid pain relievers and ADHD medications need import permit.
workarounds.tradeoff: Some Western medications are simply not allowed.

workarounds.action: If a urine test is requested at Changi, comply — refusal triggers arrest. If you are confident you are clean, the test resolves quickly. If you have used cannabis recently abroad, you are at serious risk; legal counsel should be sought immediately.
workarounds.tradeoff: Singapore Bar mandates representation but penalties are severe regardless.

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Indonesia has similar death-penalty trafficking thresholds (Bali Nine 2015) but 2021 reform softened possession; SG retains strict first-offense consequences.
UAE Federal Decree-Law 30/2021 reduced first-offense penalties to rehabilitation; SG retains caning + mandatory sentencing.
